baseline.transcript: so my room doesn't have a lot of things but the things that are actually worth mentioning includes a bed on which i sleep or relax then i could see a pillow which helps me sleep faster then there is a table on which i spend most of my hours in a day on the table there are two laptops one is quite old which i use for some minimal tasks like surfing web or running a live server the other one is the main laptop which i usually use for my day-to-day task then i could see a few registers on my table alongside a few stationery which includes pen and pencil and i could see two mouses on my table and a rubik's cube also one earphones which i hardly use. Then I could see a beanbag on my in my room on which I put my clothes then there is a AC and there's an Elmira in which I put my clothes yeah nothing much is there in my room

test.transcript: So for dividing 17 by 3, I'll start looking for a factor of 3, which is less than 17, but also nearest to 17. So I'll get 5 times 3, which is 15. So I'll subtract 15 from 17, where I'll get 2 as the remainder and 5 as the quotient. Then I still have the remainder. So I'll go further on by adding a decimal point and a 0 in the remainder. so now I look for a factor of 3 which is nearest to 20 but also less than 20 so I get 18 which is 3 times 6 now I'll put 6 in the quotient and subtract 18 from 20 from there I'll get 2 so the quotient is 5.6 and the remainder is still 2 which which makes me think that the quotient is going to be repetitive, so I'll just take the answer as 5.666 as my final answer after dividing 17 by 3.
